topic: Codeium to Windsurf: The Full History and What Changed

key_points: Founded by MIT graduates Varun Mohan and Douglas Chen in 2021 as Exafunction (GPU optimization)
Early backing from Kleiner Perkins, Greenoaks Capital, General Catalyst ($200M+)
Reached 1 million developers in just 4 months after Windsurf Editor launch (Nov 2024)
ARR hit $40M by March 2025; $243M in new funding raised at $3B valuation
OpenAI acquisition for $3B announced May 2025; covers company history well but pre-dates Cognition deal
Codeium extension lived inside VS Code or JetBrains; Windsurf Editor IS the IDE
Cascade: AI with Write mode (makes code changes) and Chat mode (context-aware Q&A)
Live preview of webpage changes before applying them
Windsurf Editor imports VS Code settings, extensions, themes, keyboard shortcuts
MCP integration pulls context from GitHub PRs, Slack, Jira, Notion
Deep founder interview with Varun Mohan on building Windsurf from scratch
Explains why they abandoned Exafunction's profitable GPU business
Growth: 0 to 1M developers in 4 months after Windsurf Editor launch
Strong on culture and decision-making; weaker on post-acquisition product changes
Comprehensive business breakdown from Contrary Research
Covers full funding history, revenue milestones, and competitive positioning
Heavy on business metrics; light on developer-facing product changes
Good sourcing for financial figures and founding story
Reviews Windsurf post-Cognition acquisition (2026)
Covers SWE-1.5 model and Codemaps as new differentiators
Compares experience before and after Cognition ownership
Addresses user concerns about plugin quality degradation during IDE transition

stats.fact: Codeium was founded in 2021 as Exafunction, a GPU optimization startup, before pivoting to AI code assistance in 2022
Codeium launched its beta coding product in October 2022 as a multi-IDE plugin
Codeium grew from under 1K users (early 2023) to over 1 million downloads by February 2024
Windsurf Editor launched November 2024 (VS Code fork with agentic Cascade AI), attracting 10K+ users in 2 days and hundreds of thousands within weeks
Codeium rebranded to Windsurf in April 2025; ARR had reached $100M — up 8x from $12M in Q4 2024
OpenAI offered ~$3B to acquire Windsurf in May 2025; deal collapsed over Microsoft IP disputes
Google agreed July 11 2025 to pay $2.4B to hire CEO Varun Mohan, CTO Douglas Chen, and ~40 employees via acquihire + technology license
Cognition AI (makers of Devin autonomous coding agent) acquired remaining Windsurf business on July 14 2025 for ~$250M
At Cognition acquisition close, Windsurf had $82M ARR, 350+ enterprise customers, and 210 employees
Cognition was valued at $10.2B in September 2025, two months after acquiring Windsurf
SWE-1.5, Cognition's proprietary model powering Windsurf, runs at 950 tokens/second — 13x faster than Claude Sonnet 4.5
Windsurf supports 40+ IDEs and 70+ programming languages; rival Cursor is VS Code-only (no JetBrains)
Windsurf pricing tiers: Free ($0, 25 credits/mo), Pro ($15/mo), Teams ($30/user/mo), Enterprise ($60/user/mo)
Windsurf has 1M+ active users and 4,000+ enterprise customers as of 2026
Codemaps: AI-annotated visual maps of code structure showing grouped sections, trace guides, and precise line-level linking — no competitor matches this

angles: The triple-pivot narrative: Exafunction (GPU) → Codeium (plugin) → Windsurf (AI-native IDE) — rare story of a startup that reinvented itself twice and succeeded each time
What actually changed for developers: concrete before/after product comparison — old Codeium plugin vs. new Windsurf IDE, plugin naming, Cascade agentic system
The $5B bidding war explained simply: how one startup attracted OpenAI, Google, AND Cognition simultaneously — and why each deal succeeded or collapsed
Windsurf's explosive growth curve: from $0 to $100M ARR in under 3 years without big tech backing — what drove adoption
The 'brand follows product' rebrand: Windsurf's name change tells the story of a philosophy shift from 'assistant' to 'partner'
Cascade vs GitHub Copilot vs Cursor Agent: where Windsurf's agentic context approach differs and why it attracted enterprise buyers
Post-Cognition reality: SWE-1.5 model, Codemaps, Devin integration — what's new in Windsurf 2026 and what it means for existing users
outline: H2: Origins: How a GPU Startup Became an AI Code Editor (2021–2022)
H2: Codeium's Rise: Building a Developer-First Plugin Empire (2023–2024)
H2: The Windsurf Editor Launch: From Plugin to Full Agentic IDE (November 2024)
H2: The Official Rebrand: Why 'Codeium' Became 'Windsurf' (April 2025)
H2: What Actually Changed for Developers — Then vs. Now
H2: The $5 Billion Drama: OpenAI, Google, and Cognition's Three-Way Split (2025)
H2: Windsurf Under Cognition: SWE-1.5, Codemaps, and Devin Integration (2026)
H2: Should You Switch from the Codeium Plugin to Windsurf IDE?
